task_completion.description: Open the target image in the GIMP application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is open with the original photograph (an Unsplash image) loaded on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Access the Filters menu in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Filters' dropdown menu is clicked and expanded in the top menu bar of GIMP.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and select the RGB Noise tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent navigates through 'Filters' -> 'Noise' and clicks on 'RGB Noise...', opening the RGB Noise dialog box.

task_completion.description: Configure the RGB Noise settings to add film grain.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the RGB Noise dialog box, the sliders for Red, Green, Blue, or correlated noise are adjusted above 0 to create a visible grain effect.

task_completion.description: Apply the noise filter to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'OK' button in the RGB Noise dialog is clicked, and the main image canvas visibly updates to show the applied film grain/noise texture.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show mouse movements, menu navigation, and dialog box interactions within GIMP, rather than terminal commands executing ImageMagick or Python scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the noise was generated using the GIMP filter and not by importing a pre-made noisy image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'RGB Noise' dialog box must be visibly opened and interacted with during the trajectory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not run the evaluation or export scripts to bypass the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show the agent manually executing 'export_noise.sh' or 'verifier.py'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP's 'Filters -> Noise -> RGB Noise' tool to add a film grain texture to an 800x533 JPEG image. To pass the system's programmatic verification, the noise must affect all RGB channels (at least 5% increase per channel) and change at least 10% of the pixels, resulting in a local variance increase of >= 2.0. The VLM should verify that the RGB Noise dialog was actively used and that the final image canvas visibly displays the added noise.
